q: How many chambers does the human heart have?
exp: The heart has two atria and two ventricles — four chambers in total.

q: Which organ produces bile to help digest fats?
exp: The liver is the largest internal organ and performs over 500 jobs.
The small intestine is about 6 metres long with millions of folds.
Red blood cells contain haemoglobin, which binds oxygen and gives them their colour.

q: What is the longest bone in the human body?
exp: The femur, in the thigh, is the longest and strongest bone.

q: Which part of the brain controls balance and coordination?
exp: The cerebellum holds more than half of the brain's neurons.

q: How many pairs of ribs do humans have?
exp: Twelve pairs of ribs protect the heart and lungs.
Insulin controls the level of sugar in the blood.

q: Which is the main muscle used for breathing?
exp: A spasm of the diaphragm causes hiccups.

q: Which vessels carry blood away from the heart?
exp: Arteries carry oxygen-rich blood; veins bring it back.
Bone marrow makes millions of blood cells every second.

q: About how many litres of blood does an adult have?
exp: Blood completes a full lap of the body in about 1 minute.

q: What is the job of white blood cells?
exp: White blood cells are the soldiers of the immune system.
